name: Okta + Generic SIEM
description: Okta as the identity provider with conditional-access policies, paired with a generic SIEM (Splunk, Chronicle, Elastic, or similar) for log analysis. A minimal-tooling stack for organizations without Microsoft E5, CrowdStrike, or Netskope. Relies heavily on network-level signals and identity-based controls.
compensating_controls: Use firewall/proxy DNS and URL filtering to block known consumer AI domains (DET-NET-001 watchlist).
Use firewall/proxy rules to block API endpoints for consumer AI services (DET-NET-002).
Implement Okta sign-on policies to restrict access from unmanaged devices to corporate SaaS applications.
Correlate firewall/proxy logs in your SIEM with Okta authentication events to identify which users are accessing AI services.
Heavy reliance on AUP enforcement, employee education, and manager talking points to compensate for limited technical detection.
Consider adding a SWG (Zscaler, Netskope) or CASB for richer inline detection as the program matures.

things_to_know.title: Limited detection depth
things_to_know.content: Without endpoint or CASB telemetry, this stack can only detect AI use at the network edge. Users on personal devices, VPNs, or mobile networks are not visible. AUP and comms are the primary control.

things_to_know.title: No DLP capability
things_to_know.content: Without a DLP tool, you cannot detect or prevent sensitive-data uploads to AI services. Compensate with network-level blocking of known AI domains and clear AUP restrictions.

things_to_know.title: SIEM correlation is manual
things_to_know.content: You will need to build custom correlation rules in your SIEM to join proxy/firewall logs with Okta identity data. Plan for rule development and tuning time.

things_to_know.title: Upgrade path recommended
things_to_know.content: This stack provides a starting point. Plan an upgrade path to a stack with CASB, endpoint DLP, and EDR for a mature Shadow AI detection program.

policy_token_replacements.dlp_tool: network egress filtering at your firewall/proxy (no inline DLP)
policy_token_replacements.casb_tool: none (compensate with proxy logs + SIEM correlation)
policy_token_replacements.swg_tool: your firewall or secure web gateway
